mnemonics.title: Virtual Functions Remember
mnemonics.content: VIRTUAL: V-irtual I-n R-untime T-hrough I-nheritance L-ike A-ll R-ecursive calls - Functions resolved at runtime using VTABLE

mnemonics.title: Early vs Late Binding
mnemonics.content: EARLY = Compile time = Static = Fast | LATE = Runtime = Dynamic = Flexible but slower

mnemonics.title: Pure Virtual Function
mnemonics.content: PURE = 0 implementation = Must override = Makes class abstract = Cannot instantiate

acronyms.usage: Lookup table containing function pointers for virtual functions in a class
Hidden pointer in each object pointing to class VTABLE for runtime resolution
Mechanism to obtain type information at runtime using typeid and dynamic_cast
Another name for VTABLE - table of virtual function pointers

visualTips.visualization: Think of VTABLE as a restaurant menu (fixed list of functions) and VPTR as a waiter's order pad (points to which menu/function to execute). The waiter (VPTR) decides at runtime which dish (function) to bring.
Early binding = phone booking (decided before arrival). Late binding = ordering from menu at restaurant (decided at the moment based on what's available).
Like cutting a 3D object - when you slice off the derived part, you lose the unique features. Only the base 2D outline remains.
Think of a remote control (base pointer) that can operate different TVs (derived objects). The same button press behaves differently based on which TV is connected.

analogies.analogy: Pre-scheduled taxi - you know which taxi will pick you up when you book it. The decision is fixed in advance.
Uber/Ola - the actual driver assigned is determined when the ride starts, not when you request it. Based on current availability.
Universal remote control - same button (virtual function call) operates different devices (objects of different classes) differently.
Blueprint of a house - you can't build a house from just a blueprint (abstract class), but you can create actual houses (concrete classes) from it.
